rules.description: TypeScript compiled via tsc to dist/ — each package has tsconfig.build.json, main field points to ./dist/index.js, pi loads extensions from dist/
Never create, copy, modify, or delete files in .pi/ — it is the user's runtime testing directory

Bundled workflow/agent/monitor specs live in their package directories: packages/pi-workflows/workflows/ for workflows, packages/pi-workflows/agents/ for agents, packages/pi-behavior-monitors/examples/ for monitor specs and templates
In pi-workflows, shared interfaces are defined in src/types.ts — other packages define types alongside their modules
Expression evaluator uses property access only — no eval(), no arithmetic/logic
Domain logic lives in YAML specs, templates, and schemas — never in the runtime
Extension owns its own subprocess dispatch — independent of pi-subagents, no shared state
JSON is the intermediate representation between steps — file system is the data bus
State persisted after each step via atomic write (tmp + rename)
{{ var }}/{% tag %} for Nunjucks prompt rendering, ${{ expr }} for workflow data flow — never mix contexts
New agent templates MUST use Nunjucks extends/block when a base template exists for their structural pattern family. Flat templates in families with established bases are defects, not shortcuts.
Agents accepting structured input MUST define inputSchema in .agent.yaml. Template variable references ({{ field }}) without a corresponding inputSchema property are silent failures.

Agents appearing in 2+ workflows MUST accept variable input shapes — identical input wiring across workflows is copy-paste, not composition. Templates must include conditionals that adapt to input variation.

Template families sharing structural patterns (read → findings → schema) MUST extract shared structure into a base template with extends/block. Duplicating prompt structure across agent templates is a defect.

test_conventions.runner_command: tsx --test src/*.test.ts (pi-project, pi-jit-agents, pi-workflows); vitest run (pi-behavior-monitors)
